published_prior_art.cite: more1983trustregion (https://doi.org/10.1137/0904038): Euclidean-ball/ellipsoid constrained least-squares and support-function dictionary

repo_prior_art.cite: none; Q1_SpectralThreshold and q1_spectral_phase_transition concern Markov FWL row-space/sign phenomena, not Honest-DiD CI(M) breakdown

published_prior_art.cite: rambachan2023parallel (https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdad018) fixed-M Honest-DiD plus breakdown reporting; more1983trustregion covers the finite-dimensional ellipsoid-distance core

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_did_anticipation_bounded/v1 is bounded anticipation support functions, not covariance-whitened aggregation loss

published_prior_art.cite: liu2025cohort (https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.01829): cohort-anchored robust inference already targets misleading aggregated Rambachan-Roth inference in staggered adoption

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The deterministic dictionary is standard support-function/trust-region convex geometry, not a novel theorem-level contribution.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses classical trust-region/point-to-ellipsoid prior art such as More-Sorensen 1983; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless a genuinely new econometric inference result is added.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Even if true, the aggregation result reads as an operator refinement of Liu 2025 cohort-anchored robust inference, not a flagship-level standalone kernel.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The whitening covariance V uses only L Sigma_post L^T even though the baseline extrapolation delta0_post is estimated from beta_hat_pre; the full covariance should include pre/post covariance after subtracting the extrapolated pretrend.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The aligned-direction formulas with sigma_max(K) and sigma_min^+(K) do not reduce correctly for an anisotropic K; if q aligns with singular axis j, the boundary generally depends on sigma_j or a secular equation, not both extremes.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Row(L S_A) / Row(L A S_A) is not well-defined as written because L is defined on aggregated post coordinates, while S_A is the pre-aggregation sensitivity matrix.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace V by the covariance of the actual debiased target, including the linear map from pre-period estimates into delta0_post and all pre/post covariance terms.
Withdraw the sigma_max/sigma_min aligned formulas and state the general threshold through the standard trust-region secular equation; keep scalar r=1 as the clean closed-form corner.
For aggregation, introduce distinct unaggregated and aggregated target-loading maps so the row-space quotient lives in a single ambient space.

To pursue flagship tier, add a genuinely econometric result beyond ellipsoid geometry, such as uniform inference for the estimated breakdown threshold or a sharp comparison to Liu-style cohort-anchored robust inference over an open class of staggered designs.

recommended_next_step: Reject this v1 as a flagship proposal; redraft around a corrected covariance/secular-equation statement and either accept field-tier scope or pivot to a stronger inference/aggregation theorem.
